calendar.matters:
CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Mar-02-2026 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And Consent Judgment
Off Calendar Feb-25-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF GABRIEL ESPINOZA'S Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And Consent Judgment.

The court grants the unopposed motion to approve the Proposition 65 settlement and consent judgment.

The court finds that the warning required by the settlement is in compliance with Proposition 65 (Health & Safety Code sections 25249.5 to 25249.14); that the award of attorney's fees pursuant to the settlement is reasonable under California law; and that, considering the factors set out in Health & Safety Code section 25249.7, subdivision (b)(2), and the facts asserted in the moving papers, the penalty amount is reasonable. Accordingly the motion is granted and the parties may submit the proposed judgment for signature.

Proposed order and consent judgment are submitted.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And Consent Judgment

Judge: Christine Van Aken. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not Reported. (301/CVA)

CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jan-07-2026 Continued To Mar-10-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And Consent Judgment
Off Calendar Dec-16-2025 Continued To Jan-22-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 301
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF GABRIEL ESPINOZA'S Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And Consent Judgment.

On December 2, 2025, pursuant to the parties' stipulation, the court continued the hearing on Plaintiff's motion to December 16, 2025. The court directed the parties to file a joint statement regarding the status of settlement and proposed consent judgment no later than December 9, 2025 (five court days in advance of the hearing date). The parties failed to comply with the court's order to provide an update regarding the status of settlement.

Therefore, on the court's own motion, the matter is continued to January 22, 2026 for hearing. The parties shall update the court about the status of settlement and the proposed consent order in a joint filing no later than five court days in advance of the continued hearing date.

Visiting Judge: Cindee Mayfield; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CM)
